goals: Each node exposes its effect-specific parameters as native ComfyUI FLOAT widgets.
Each node outputs `EFFECT_PARAMS` with the effect name and current parameter values.
Each node shows a live animated GLSL preview that updates in real time when a parameter widget changes.

userStories.title: CoolGlitchEffect node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI user, I want a dedicated Glitch effect node with wave controls so that I can tune the glitch look and wire the result to the video generator.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Native FLOAT inputs: `wave_freq` (default 120.0, min 1.0, max 500.0, step 1.0), `wave_amp` (default 0.0025, min 0.0, max 0.05, step 0.0005), `speed` (default 10.0, min 0.0, max 100.0, step 0.5).
`execute(self, wave_freq, wave_amp, speed)` returns `(build_effect_params("glitch", {"u_wave_freq": wave_freq, "u_wave_amp": wave_amp, "u_speed": speed}),)`.
The node widget shows a live animated preview of `glitch.frag` using the placeholder texture.

userStories.title: CoolVHSEffect node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI user, I want a dedicated VHS effect node with scanline and chroma controls so that I can dial in the retro look precisely.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Native FLOAT inputs: `scanline_intensity` (default 0.04, min 0.0, max 0.5, step 0.005), `jitter_amount` (default 0.0018, min 0.0, max 0.02, step 0.0002), `chroma_shift` (default 0.002, min 0.0, max 0.02, step 0.0002).
`execute(self, scanline_intensity, jitter_amount, chroma_shift)` returns the correct `EFFECT_PARAMS` bundle with keys `u_scanline_intensity`, `u_jitter_amount`, `u_chroma_shift`.
The node widget shows a live animated preview of `vhs.frag` using the placeholder texture.

userStories.title: CoolZoomPulseEffect node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI user, I want a dedicated ZoomPulse effect node with pulse controls so that I can control zoom intensity and rhythm.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Native FLOAT inputs: `pulse_amp` (default 0.06, min 0.0, max 0.5, step 0.005), `pulse_speed` (default 3.0, min 0.1, max 20.0, step 0.1).
`execute(self, pulse_amp, pulse_speed)` returns the correct `EFFECT_PARAMS` bundle with keys `u_pulse_amp`, `u_pulse_speed`.
The node widget shows a live animated preview of `zoom_pulse.frag` using the placeholder texture.

userStories.title: Real-time preview reflects parameter changes
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI user, I want the preview canvas to update when I change a parameter widget so that I can preview the effect before queuing a render.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Each per-effect node's JS extension registers an `onWidgetChanged` handler that calls `renderer.set_uniform(uniform_name, value)` and does not reload the shader program.
The preview updates within one animation frame of the widget value changing.
The mapping from widget name to uniform name matches the keys used in `execute` (e.g. widget `wave_freq` → uniform `u_wave_freq`).
When WebGL2 is unavailable, the overlay error message is shown and no exception is thrown.

functionalRequirements.description: All parameter inputs are defined as native ComfyUI `FLOAT` widgets in `INPUT_TYPES["required"]`.

A new JS file `web/effect_node_widget.js` exports `mount_effect_node_widget(node, effect_name, param_specs)` — a reusable function that mounts only the preview canvas DOM widget and registers an `onWidgetChanged` hook that calls `renderer.set_uniform` for each parameter widget change.

Each effect has a corresponding JS extension file (`web/glitch_effect.js`, etc.) that calls `mount_effect_node_widget` in `onNodeCreated` with the correct `effect_name` and `param_specs` (array of `{widget_name, uniform_name}`).

`mount_effect_node_widget` initializes the WebGL2 preview with default uniform values from `DEFAULT_PARAMS` (fetched via the existing `/cool_effects/shaders` endpoint or hardcoded in JS matching the Python defaults).
The `CoolEffectSelector` node and its widget are not modified.
